analysis.target_garment_description: A beige single-breasted mac coat with a classic collar, button-front closure, two welt pockets, and buttoned cuff tabs.
preserved_details: long sleeves with adjustable cuff tabs

visual_details.description: Classic pointed collar
Long sleeves with adjustable button tabs at the cuffs
Two horizontal welt pockets on the front
Five dark brown buttons for the main closure (one at collar, four on placket) and one dark brown button on each cuff tab
Button-front closure

evidence.observation: Image shows a coat garment.
The garment is predominantly beige in color.
The garment has a single-breasted design and extends to mid-thigh length, consistent with a mac coat silhouette.
A classic pointed collar is visible.
Long sleeves are present, ending in cuff tabs with buttons.
Two welt pockets are located on the lower front of the coat.
The front closure features five dark brown buttons, including one at the collar and four down the placket.
Each cuff has an adjustable tab secured with a dark brown button.

analysis.target_garment_description: The only visible garment, an olive green wrap dress.
analysis.silhouette_summary: A-line wrap dress with V-neck, short flutter sleeves, elasticized tie waist, and a midi-length curved hem.

visual_details.description: V-neckline
Short flutter sleeves
Wrap front with tie belt at waist
Elasticized waist seam
Midi length skirt with curved, overlapping hem
Soft, flowy fabric texture with slight sheen

evidence.observation: A single dress is visible in the image, fully present and clearly identifiable.
The garment's primary color is olive green.

The garment features a wrap-style bodice and skirt, a V-neckline, short flutter sleeves, an elasticized waist with a tie belt, and an A-line skirt that ends at midi length with a curved, overlapping hem.
The entire garment is visible within the image frame, with no parts cropped.
All essential regions of the garment are clearly visible without significant occlusion.
The neckline forms a V-shape.
The sleeves are short, extending just over the shoulders, and have a loose, flared 'flutter' appearance.
The front of the dress is designed to wrap, secured by a tie belt knotted at the waist.
The waistline shows gathering and a distinct seam, indicating elastication.
The skirt extends below the knee and features a distinct curved, overlapping hemline typical of a wrap style.
The fabric exhibits a soft drape, subtle wrinkles, and a slight reflective quality, suggesting a flowy material.
